channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump, desafiante tras una mala semana, insiste en sus ideas políticamente impopulares
item.description: El presidente Trump continúa actuando como si fuera políticamente todopoderoso, incluso ante los indicios de que no lo es.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump en la Casa Blanca el viernes.

item.title: Un nuevo decreto talibán formaliza el matrimonio infantil, advierte la ONU
item.description: Una nueva ley afgana exige que las niñas esperen hasta la pubertad antes de intentar salir de un matrimonio. También exige la mediación para las mujeres que intentan escapar de un esposo maltratador.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una farmacia en Kabul, Afganistán, el martes. El gobierno talibán ha impuesto algunas de las restricciones más duras del mundo a mujeres y niñas.

item.title: 6 consejos de expertos para dormir si sufres alergias estacionales
item.description: Cuando cuesta respirar, cuesta descansar.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Schupak

item.title: As Easy as Riding a Bike? Adult Learners Give It a Try.
item.description: At Bike New York’s free classes for adults, many novice riders are nervous. Many are embarrassed. And many are women.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Rosenfield and Elias Williams

media:content.media:credit: Elias Williams for The New York Times

item.title: ‘I Was Prepared for a Long Wait and Surly Interactions’
item.description: A trip to the D.M.V., an unusual place to buy fruit and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: New York’s Rape Laws Leave Out Many Victims Who Drank Willingly
item.description: Prosecutors say accusers who chose to drink or get high have a “very high burden” of proving they were physically helpless and unable to consent. There is a push to change the law.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer and Olivia Bensimon

media:content.media:credit: Bethany Mollenkof for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Leslie Hunt said she was raped in October 2015 when she went out with a co-worker for drinks. She woke up in a Brooklyn hospital.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 24, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iranian Officials Say Proposed Peace Deal Would Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has not formally responded to President Trump’s announcement that a peace deal was close. But officials suggested the oil choke point in the Strait of Hormuz could reopen.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A crowd waving Iranian flags during a mass wedding ceremony in Tehran last week.

item.title: Kyiv, Ukraine, Hit in Russian Missile Attack
item.description: Buildings rattled in the Ukrainian capital for hours early Sunday. It was unclear if Russia’s Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missile had been used.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:description: The sky lit up from the glow of fires during a Russian strike on the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, early Sunday.

item.title: YouTube tiene un problema con la piratería hecha con IA
item.description: Narradas de forma robótica, copias ilegales generadas con IA de ‘Los juegos del hambre’ y otros títulos exitosos son cada vez más comunes en la plataforma.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter

media:content.media:credit: Donald Johnson para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El escritor John Grisham es uno de los que piden mayores esfuerzos para frenar la proliferación de audiolibros pirateados en YouTube.

item.title: The 15-Year-Old Keeping War Memories Alive
item.description: Luke Morrison may be the youngest person keeping alive an age-old tradition: to process war through the memories and mementos of those who experienced it.
item.dc:creator: Jasper Craven and Amina Gingold
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Amina Gingold for The New York Times

item.title: I Need an Escape From Travel
item.description: If I cannot get an upgrade — and I cannot, as I have no status, in any aspect of my life — I would like us to suffer more equally when we travel.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Feintzeig

item.title: The War-Driven Supply Shock Already Roiling Manufacturing in Asia
item.description: A shortage of naphtha, stemming from the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, is disrupting manufacturing and retail goods in Japan and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Hisako Ueno
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: The Japanese food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its potato chips.

item.title: Can Concrete Molds Revive Coral Reefs Ruined by Bombs and Climate Change?
item.description: In a devastated section of the Coral Triangle in the Pacific Ocean, a conservation group is trying to build an artificial reef.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed and Sirachai Arunrugstichai

media:content.media:description: Robin Philippo, the managing director of the Tropical Research and Conservation Center, installing an artificial reef structure near Pom Pom Island in Malaysia.

item.title: ‘Debbie Downer’ Is Enjoying an Upswing
item.description: With her popular mystical podcast, a return to Broadway in “The Rocky Horror Show” and a second Tony nomination, Rachel Dratch is finally getting her due.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat

media:content.media:credit: OK McCausland for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: May 24, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, May 24, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Europe Has Interest in Talking Peace With Putin, but Questions Remain
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, May 24, 2026.

item.title: A Refuge in the Arctic
item.description: A postcard from the northernmost church on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:description: The Rev. Siv Limstrand, wearing sealskin.

item.title: Three Drake Albums, Three Songs of the Week
item.description: On this triple-track edition of the “Popcast” Song of the Week, our critic Jon Caramanica unpacks the themes of Drake’s latest era.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Iran Agreed to Give Up Enriched Uranium in Deal Announced by Trump, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Precise details were unclear but the officials said the U.S. demanded a commitment from Iran on uranium as part of any initial agreement.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard in Tehran depicts the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Many Questions, Few Details in Latest Iran Peace Proposal
item.description: It is too early to tell what exactly Trump and Iran have agreed to, or if they have agreed to much at all.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump in New York on Friday.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Close to Peace Agreement With Iran
item.description: President Trump said on Saturday the United States was close to reaching a deal with Iran toward ending the war there. The deal, which he said would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, was subject to finalization by the U.S., Iran, and other countries.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A poster of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former supreme leader, in Tehran this month. He was killed in an airstrike at the start of the war.

item.title: Britain Preparing for Mission That Could Clear Strait of Hormuz
item.description: At Gibraltar, at the tip of Spain, British forces are ready to deploy autonomous mine-hunting equipment if a peace agreement is achieved.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Royal Fleet Auxiliary Lyme Bay in the port of Gibraltar in 2014. The British military plans to send the amphibious landing ship to help secure the Strait of Hormuz if and when the war with Iran ends.

item.title: Gunman in Shooting Near White House Killed in Exchange of Fire With Secret Service
item.description: A gunman known to the U.S. Secret Service opened fire near the White House and was shot and killed by federal officers. President Trump was in the White House at the time.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Clarence Williams

media:content.media:description: Gunfire broke out near 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue, at the northwestern corner of the White House grounds.

item.title: Next, Please!
item.description: David Steinberg returns to the Sunday puzzle with a new switcheroo.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: This puzzle’s delicate theme rewards close observation.

item.title: Director Cristian Mungiu’s ‘Fjord’ Wins Palme d’Or at Cannes Film Festival
item.description: The film, from the Romanian director Cristian Mungiu, explores the tension between religious conservatism and social liberalism. The award closed a festival that felt comparatively muted to many.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

media:content.media:description: The director Cristian Mungiu, center, accepted the Palme d’Or in Cannes, France, on Saturday.

item.title: 40,000 Ordered to Evacuate in California Amid Toxic Chemical Tank Crisis
item.description: Firefighters worked on Saturday at the GKN Aerospace aircraft components plant in Garden Grove, Calif., to stop a storage tank filled with a toxic chemical from failing, risking a major explosion or a toxic spill.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:credit: Jeff Gritchen/The Orange County Register, via AP
media:content.media:description: The crisis occurred at GKN Aerospace, which builds engines and landing gear, in Garden Grove, Calif., about 35 miles outside of Los Angeles.

item.title: Graduating Into A.I. Pessimism
item.description: Backlash to artificial intelligence is here, and digital natives are leading the charge. The Opinion columnist Michelle Goldberg argues that the A.I. pessimism in America stems from a lack of responsible regulation.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg and Ingrid Holmquist

item.title: Investigators Look Into Staten Island Blasts That Killed Shipyard Worker
item.description: The explosions at May Ship Repair Contracting Corporation, which erupted after a fire on a dock, killed one of the company’s subcontractors, a representative said.
item.dc:creator: Nate Schweber and Claire Fahy

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.description: PresidTrump provided few details about the preliminary agreement, which he said was “largely negotiated.”
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Grizz Chapman, Actor Known for His Role on ’30 Rock,’ Dies at 52
item.description: Mr. Chapman, who played a member of Tracy Morgan’s entourage on the show, died on Friday, according to his cousin.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz

media:content.media:description: Grizzwald Chapman in 2012 in New York City.

item.title: Colbert’s Sudden Return to Late Night? It Happened on Public Access.
item.description: A day after the comedian hosted the final episode of “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” on CBS, he hosted “Only in Monroe,” a community-access program in Monroe, Mich.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Dozens Killed in Coal Mine Explosion, China State Media Says
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China killed dozens on Friday, according to Xinhua, a Chinese state news agency. In 2024, China’s National Mine Safety Administration cited the coal mine for “severe safety hazards” and high gas levels. As of Saturday morning, the cause of the explosion was still unknown, according to CCTV, the Chinese state broadcaster.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Rescuers at the site of a deadly explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in China’s Shanxi Province on Saturday.

item.title: Los cubanoestadounidenses de Florida aumentan la ayuda a sus familiares en Cuba
item.description: Meses después del inicio del bloqueo petrolero impuesto por el presidente Trump, muchos cubanos dependen más que nunca de sus familiares en EE. UU. para cubrir sus necesidades diarias.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams, Andrea Zarate and David Ovalle

media:content.media:credit: Saul Martinez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los habitantes del sur de Florida se están movilizando para enviar cajas llenas de carne enlatada, bolsas de arroz y frijoles y otros alimentos básicos a familiares hambrientos.

item.title: Trump Is Setting His Sights on Restricting Legal Immigration
item.description: After a year focused on immigrants here illegally, the Trump administration is now making it harder for legal migrants to stay in the country. It is a risky pivot.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:description: The Trump administration’s decision to force immigrants to go abroad to await their green card application decisions clarified a new push.

item.title: Brooklyn Man Wedged in Upstate New York Cave Is Rescued After 6 Hours
item.description: A cave explorer got stuck in a narrow crevice and developed hypothermia. Rescuers brought him miniature Reese’s Peanut Butter Cups to keep his energy up.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:description: Rescuers on Sunday helped a cave explorer who was stuck in a subterranean passageway at Merlins Cave in Canaan, N.Y.

item.title: France Bans Israeli Minister Over Treatment of Detained Flotilla Activists
item.description: Itamar Ben-Gvir, the Israeli national security minister, has long drawn criticism for his hard-line statements and harsh policies against Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman

media:content.media:description: Israel’s national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, in the Israeli Parliament on Wednesday.

item.title: Garden Grove Chemical Tank Could Be Inching Toward Explosion, Authorities Say
item.description: Firefighters still have few options to prevent a tank of toxic chemicals in Garden Grove, Calif., from leaking or exploding.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel

media:content.media:description: Water is sprayed onto a chemical storage tank at risk of exploding in Garden Grove, Calif.

item.title: Trump Administration Chips Away at Last Traces of Broad Inquiry Into Jan. 6
item.description: Two recent moves aligned the department ever more closely with President Trump’s own efforts to whitewash the events of that day.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Trump during the breach of the Capitol on January 6, 2021.

item.title: Inside the $1.8 Billion MAGA Payout Fund
item.description: Americans should be cleareyed about what President Trump is doing: He is taking $1.8 billion worth of taxpayer money and rewarding loyalists willing to defy the law and commit violence. The Times editorial board explains in this video.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board and Stephanie Shen

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson Celebrate Marriage on Bahamas Private Island
item.description: After getting legally married in Palm Beach, the couple exchanged vows in the Caribbean this weekend, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson in Palm Beach, Fla., in February.

item.title: A Lull in Fighting Gave Time to Bury Their Dead
item.description: In the relative calm of the early days of a cease-fire, there were heart-rending scenes as people exhumed their loved ones from temporary graves and reburied them.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder and Christina Goldbaum

media:content.media:description: The human toll of the war has spared few parts of southern Lebanon.

item.title: Husband of Prominent New York Gallerist Convicted in Murder-for-Hire Plot
item.description: Brent Sikkema, who championed artists like Kara Walker, was found stabbed to death in his apartment in Brazil in 2024 amid divorce proceedings with his spouse.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small

media:content.media:description: The entrance to the apartment in Rio de Janeiro where the art dealer Brent Sikkema was reported to be found stabbed to death in 2024.

item.title: Garden Grove Chemical Plant Crisis: What to Know and Evacuation Map
item.description: Firefighters continue to cool a chemical storage tank with water to prevent it from breaching or exploding. Some 40,000 residents near the Orange County plant have been evacuated to shelters.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: The GKN Aerospace site where a chemical storage tank is at risk of exploding.

item.title: What to Know About Lifeguards as New York City’s Beaches Open for Summer
item.description: City beaches are back open, though outdoor pools won’t open for another month. Here’s where things stand at the unofficial start of summer.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Yuvraj Khanna for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York City has 370 lifeguards ready to staff beaches, and it expects the number to grow as more lifeguards complete certification in the coming weeks.

item.title: Israeli Strikes Pummel Lebanon, Killing Medics Amid Fragile Truce
item.description: Saturday’s strikes damaged a main hospital in the Lebanese city of Tyre, as funerals for paramedics killed a day earlier were held.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaayto and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Mourners at the funeral of a paramedic and two civil defense workers, in Tyre, Lebanon, on Saturday. They were killed in an Israeli airstrike the day before, according to Lebanon’s health ministry.

item.title: A Fresher, Brighter Macaroni Salad
item.description: Alexa Weibel’s macaroni salad with lemon and herbs complements anything off the grill (and will get you compliments).
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Yossy Arefi for The New York Times (Photography and Styling)
media:content.media:description: Alexa Weibel’s macaroni salad with lemon and herbs.

item.title: Alexandros Giotopoulos of November 17, Greek Militant Group, Is Freed
item.description: The release of Alexandros Giotopoulos was denounced by relatives of people killed by November 17, a Greek far-left group that was active between 1975 and 2002.
item.dc:creator: Niki Kitsantonis

media:content.media:description: Alexandros Giotopoulos in a courtroom in an Athens prison in 2005. His lawyer said he was released on Thursday because of his age, among other reasons.

item.title: These Are a Few of My Favorite Picnic Treats
item.description: Chewy brownie cookies, peanut butter blackberry bars and pistachio halvah Rice Krispies treats travel well and hold up in the sun.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Yossy Arefi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dawn Perry’s peanut butter blackberry bars.

item.title: Cómo disminuir el estrés con 3 preguntas
item.description: Conseguir una perspectiva más real sobre las dificultades de la vida suele reducir la ansiedad.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn
